checks.a_analysis: The content in the provided sections matches the source document's text and structure. The AI-generated section titles (e.g., 'Blank Page') are permitted under the acceptable changes guidelines.
The only URL present in the document (on page 18) was verified against the source PDF and matches exactly.

A significant portion of the document is missing from the output. Specifically, Page 3 (Table of Contents), Page 4 (Blank Page), and major sections from Pages 5 through 9 (including Introduction, Accomplishments, Criminal Justice and Public Safety, Child and Family Safety, and large parts of Fraud and Compliance) are absent. The JSON explicitly acknowledges this with a placeholder: '5 sections omitted for token budget'.
checks.b_evidence_source: The entire contents of Pages 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, and 9, as well as the 'Introduction' and 'Accomplishments' sections from Page 5.
checks.b_evidence_location: sections array, index 3 (between 'Legislative Reporting Requirement' and 'Health Care')

checks.a_analysis: The table 'NC GDAC Project Contribution by SAS' on report page 14 (PDF page 16) contains all 8 rows found in the source document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The sections that are present (Legislative Reporting Requirement, Health Care, Appendix A, and Appendix B) appear to be largely complete relative to their counterparts in the source. The missing content is instead handled as complete missing sections (C3).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the 'Division of Medicaid Assistance - Medicaid Analytics Pilot' section, the Medicaid expenditure figure is malformed in the output. The source document lists the figure as '$13,808,641,305', whereas the output contains '$13,8086,413,305', which changes the numerical value.
checks.b_evidence_location: Division of Medicaid Assistance - Medicaid Analytics Pilot (under Health Care section), first paragraph

checks.a_analysis: The output duplicates section heading text. In sections 10 and 11, the heading text ('Challenges' and 'GDAC Public Private Partnership') appears both as the 'title' property of the section and as a heading element ('type': 'heading') within the elements list. In the source PDF, these text blocks appear only once as section headers.
checks.b_evidence_source: Challenges (Heading, Page 15); GDAC Public Private Partnership (Heading, Page 16)
checks.b_evidence_output: "title": "Challenges" and "type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Challenges" (Section 10); "title": "GDAC Public Private Partnership" and "type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "GDAC Public Private Partnership" (Section 11)

checks.a_analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and incorrect compared to the source document's organization and Table of Contents. In the 'Health Care' section, sibling sub-sections ('State Health Plan', 'Division of Medicaid Assistance', and 'North Carolina Health Information Exchange') are assigned inconsistent levels (H3 vs H4), suggesting a parent-child relationship that does not exist. Similarly, 'Governmental Budgetary Transparency...' is assigned H3 while its siblings in the 'Longitudinal and Performance Systems' section are assigned H4.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table of Contents (Page 3) and corresponding section headers on pages 8-12.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "State Health Plan..." (H3, ID: heading-14); "text": "Division of Medicaid..." (H4, ID: heading-15); "text": "Governmental Budgetary..." (H3, ID: heading-21)
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 9, index 7; Section 9, index 8; Section 9, index 21

checks.a_analysis: The table 'NC GDAC Project Contribution by SAS' (Page 16) is correctly converted. The structure follows the source with two columns, properly identified header rows, and data rows. Bold text for summary rows (Total, State Payments, SAS Investment) is preserved in the content, and footnotes are extracted as separate paragraphs following the table.

The reading order of the converted document faithfully follows the logical flow of the source PDF, proceeding from the cover page through sections, appendices, and the final portfolio chart in the correct sequence.

Lists throughout the document (e.g., Legislative Reporting Requirements, Pilot areas of focus, NC ECIDS activities) are captured as semantic list structures. While some numbers are included in the item text (e.g., focus areas 1-5), the overall structural meaning and nesting (particularly in Appendix A) are preserved.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the heading count in the semantic.json (30) and the rendered HTML (41). This difference of 11 headings exceeds the allowable threshold of 3 headings (10% of 30), indicating that the renderer has likely added or duplicated headings during the conversion process.
checks.b_evidence_source: Heading count in source is approximately 30 headings.
checks.b_evidence_output: Headings in semantic.json: 30; Headings in rendered HTML: 41

checks.a_analysis: The link count reported in the JSON metadata (1) is inconsistent with both the source document (which contains at least 3 URLs on pages 9 and 18) and the rendered HTML (14 links). The JSON is missing two critical government/news links found on page 9, and the HTML output significantly inflates the link count, suggesting link injection or fabrication.
checks.b_evidence_source: URLs on page 9 (bizjournals.com, gcn.com) and page 18 (ncleg.net).
checks.b_evidence_output: Links in semantic.json: 1; Links in rendered HTML: 14
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML Metadata and Section 9 (missing links), Section 12 (one link present)

checks.a_analysis: There are two major layout issues. First, the Appendix B section, which in the source PDF (page 19) consists of a comprehensive visual Gantt chart/timeline, is rendered in the HTML only as a set of headers with no following content or visual representation. Second, the visual flow of the cover page is significantly disrupted; the Table of Contents (originally on page 3) is injected immediately after the title on page 1, splitting the cover page content (Logo, 'Information Technology', and committee lists) and forcing a user to scroll through the TOC to find the rest of the cover information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 19 contains a full-page 'GDAC Activities - Calendar Year' timeline chart. Page 1 layout flows from Title to Logo to Committee list to Officers/Date.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Appendix B - GDAC Portfolio' section ends after the sub-header 'GDAC Activities - Calendar Year' with no chart content. The cover page title is immediately followed by the 'Contents' link list, displacing the rest of the cover page information.

checks.a_analysis: The financial table 'NC GDAC Project Contribution by SAS' from page 16 is rendered correctly in the HTML. It maintains a clear tabular structure with borders, distinct headers, and properly aligned data cells, making it easily readable and understandable for a sighted user.

Multiple meaningful images are missing from the rendered output. The state agency logo (NC with pine tree) on the cover page is absent. Furthermore, the complex Gantt chart graphic that comprises the entirety of Appendix B (Page 19) is completely missing, resulting in a loss of the primary data intended for that section.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 features the 'NC Information Technology' tree logo. Page 19 is a full-page graphical Gantt chart showing the GDAC Activities portfolio.
checks.b_evidence_output: The NC logo is missing between the document title and the 'Information Technology' text. The Appendix B section shows only headers with no graphical content.

checks.a_analysis: No instances of visually duplicated content blocks, paragraphs, or tables were observed in the rendered HTML output.

The overall visual quality is significantly degraded by the omission of the document's primary graphical data (the Appendix B portfolio timeline) and the lack of official branding on the cover page. The fragmentation of the cover page layout further reduces the professional appearance of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source is a professionally formatted government report with consistent branding and visual data displays.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output is missing key visual data components and branding, resulting in an incomplete and fragmented appearance.
